opportunity.title: Show HN: Output.ai - OSS framework we extracted from 500+ production AI agents
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Most OSS agent frameworks struggle with context window management across parallel tool calls—our internal approach uses a token-budget-aware scheduler that backfills work when a sub-agent hits its limit. A real gotcha: the framework layer often becomes the bottleneck, not the LLM itself, so distributed tracing at the tool invocation level is more valuable than per-call latency monitoring.
actions.reasoning: I have hands-on experience building multi-provider LLM routing systems and production AI agent frameworks, including tool orchestration and self-improving loops.
